docs.description: AVFoundation camera capture with Prism-native types — photo and video capture, flash control, and quality settings.

docs.description: Stream large responses with chunked transfer encoding and AsyncStream.

docs.description: Generate HTML responses with Swift string interpolation and response helpers.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.description: Structured error responses, global error middleware, and RFC 7807 Problem Details.

docs.title: Middleware
docs.description: Intercept, transform, and compose request/response processing with the middleware pipeline.

docs.title: Request
docs.description: Access headers, body, parameters, and parsed data from incoming HTTP requests.

docs.description: Build HTTP responses with JSON, HTML, redirects, streaming, and custom headers.

docs.description: Define URL patterns, extract parameters, and organize routes into groups.

docs.description: Configure and run your PrismHTTPServer — the foundation of every Prism application.

docs.description: Version your database schema with reversible up/down migrations.

docs.title: Relationships
docs.description: Define hasMany, belongsTo, and hasOne relationships between your models.

docs.title: Observability
docs.description: Performance tracing, structured logging, crash reporting, analytics funnels, and network inspection.

docs.title: Core Protocols
docs.description: Base protocols that all Prism modules build on — PrismEntity, PrismError, PrismMock, PrismLogger, and PrismSystemLogger.

docs.title: Resource System
docs.description: Typed string resources, image resources, and structured log messages used across Prism modules.

docs.title: Utilities

docs.description: Set up the interactive GraphQL playground to explore and test your API in the browser.

docs.description: Define your GraphQL schema with Prism's native type system — no code generation, no external tools.

docs.title: Metrics
docs.description: Track request counts, latency, error rates, and popular endpoints.

docs.title: Apple Intelligence
docs.description: Generate text using Apple's FoundationModels framework with system models, adapters, and streaming.

docs.title: NLP & RAG
docs.description: Sentiment analysis, named entity extraction, embeddings, and retrieval-augmented generation — all on-device.

docs.description: Unified ML/AI client supporting on-device CoreML, Apple Intelligence, and remote LLM backends.

docs.title: On-Device Training
docs.description: Train text and tabular ML models on-device using CreateML with zero network dependencies.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.description: Prism is a full-stack Swift ecosystem with zero third-party dependencies. Build servers, iOS/macOS apps, ML pipelines, and more — using only Apple frameworks.

docs.title: MCP Overview
docs.description: Build Model Context Protocol servers that let AI assistants like Claude use your tools, read your resources, and follow your prompts.

docs.title: Resources
docs.description: Expose data as readable resources that AI assistants can access — config files, database records, documentation.

docs.title: Tools
docs.description: Register tools that AI assistants can call to perform actions — query databases, call APIs, process data.

docs.title: Transports
docs.description: Deploy your MCP server over stdio for CLI tools or HTTP/SSE for web applications.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.description: Protect routes with bearer tokens, sessions, and custom auth strategies.

docs.title: Compression
docs.description: Compress HTTP responses to reduce bandwidth using Apple's Compression framework.

docs.title: CORS
docs.description: Configure Cross-Origin Resource Sharing for browser-based API consumers.

docs.title: Lifecycle Hooks
docs.description: Fine-grained request, response, and error hooks for cross-cutting concerns.

docs.title: Logging
docs.description: Log every request and response with timing information.

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.description: Protect your API from abuse with token bucket rate limiting.

docs.title: Advanced Rate Limiting
docs.description: Sliding window rate limiting with per-user limits, custom keys, and standard headers.

docs.title: Advanced Features
docs.description: Request deduplication, offline queues, multipart uploads, upload progress tracking, and a typed GraphQL client.

docs.title: Caching & Retry
docs.description: Actor-based LRU response caching and configurable retry policies with exponential backoff and linear strategies.

docs.description: Real-time bidirectional communication with PrismNetworkSocketClient, type-safe endpoints, and command protocols.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.description: Build a complete REST API with database, authentication, and validation in under 15 minutes.

docs.title: Event Bus
docs.description: Typed publish/subscribe event system for decoupled server components.

docs.title: Server-Sent Events
docs.description: Push real-time updates to browsers over HTTP with Server-Sent Events.

docs.title: WebSocket
docs.description: Build real-time features with native WebSocket support — RFC 6455 compliant.

docs.title: WebSocket Rooms
docs.description: Named channels, broadcasting, and presence tracking for multi-room real-time apps.

docs.title: Sessions & Cookies
docs.description: Manage user sessions with signed cookies and pluggable storage backends.

docs.title: Validation

docs.description: Chainable response assertions for clean, expressive test code.

docs.title: Request Builder

docs.title: Test Client

docs.title: Advanced UI

docs.title: Core Components
docs.description: PrismUI's primitive components — buttons, cards, text fields, avatars, progress bars, and more — all themed and accessible.

docs.title: Composite Components
docs.description: Higher-level components — sheets, carousels, charts, dashboards, and communication views built from PrismUI primitives.

docs.title: Layout & Navigation

docs.title: Theming
docs.description: PrismUI's theming system uses semantic color tokens and protocol-based themes for consistent, adaptive styling across all platforms.

docs.title: Video
